name: St. Francis River
difficultyRating: Class II-IV (upper whitewater); calmer below Sam A. Baker SP
description: Missouri's only whitewater river. The upper St. Francis through Millstream Gardens / Silver Mines (the Tiemann Shut-ins) is a 2.3-mile, rain-dependent Class II–IV pool-drop run that spikes and drops fast (USFS; American Whitewater #2921). Below the whitewater it mellows and floats more like a normal stream toward Sam A. Baker State Park and Wappapello Lake.
managingAuthority: Missouri Department of Conservation (Millstream Gardens CA) and USDA Forest Service, Mark Twain NF (Silver Mines Rec Area); streambed navigability per Missouri law

accessLaw.value: Missouri follows the "navigable in fact" doctrine: the public may float and wade streams navigable in fact, but streambeds/banks are frequently privately owned, so access is legal from public road crossings, public land, and established accesses, while portaging or camping on private gravel bars can be trespass. Navigability is decided stream by stream; there is no statewide list. (Shared Missouri paragraph — confirm the AGO/MDC citation once for the state.)
accessLaw.source: https://mdc.mo.gov/ (MDC stream access) — confirm AGO citation once for MO

gauges.name: St. Francis River near Roselle, MO
gauges.knownBias: VERIFIED — CRITICAL: USGS 07034000 discharge (00060) record ENDS 1997-09-30; the USGS gauge is discontinued. Live stage is available via NWS ROZM7 only (this is what the Eddy card's 'NOAA Gauge Details' uses). PROVIDER SET TO 'nws' (LID ROZM7), poll param 00065 gage height (ft) — the runnable ladder is in ft. Confirm the FlowProvider routes provider='nws' to ROZM7. USACE MVS St. Louis (SF_tab) is a fallback.

gauges.name: St. Francis River near Patterson, MO
gauges.knownBias: CANDIDATE, NOT primary-verified (USGS page 403'd; co-located with NWS PAZM7). Long-record gauge; discharge(00060)+gage height(00065); USACE St. Louis District coop. Represents the lower river toward Wappapello.

gauges.name: St. Francis River near Saco, MO
gauges.knownBias: VERIFIED, ACTIVE (uv 00060 1990→2026, 00065 2007→2026). Mid gauge between Roselle and Patterson — candidate representative for the transition below the shut-ins; confirm which reach it best serves.

gauges.name: St. Francis River at Wappapello, MO
gauges.knownBias: VERIFIED, ACTIVE (uv 00060 1994→2026). At Wappapello Lake — below the floated reaches; reference/limit only, not a float representative.

sections.name: Upper Whitewater (Millstream Gardens to Silver Mines / Tiemann Shut-ins)
sections.description: The reason to add this river: a 2.3-mile Class II–IV rain-fed pool-drop run through the Tiemann Shut-ins. Only whitewater on a Missouri river. Runnable mainly in spring high water; spikes and drops fast; committing at high flow. The condition badge here is a paddler runnable-range, not a lazy-float curve.

representativeGauge.rationale: American Whitewater #2921 quotes the runnable range against the Roselle gauge; MWA keys race go/no-go to a St. Francis level display
representativeGauge.knownBias: gauge site # unverified; historical funding-driven outages

thresholds.source: Eddy gauge display for 'St. Francis River near Roselle' (Stage Height ft; source: NOAA Gauge Details), hand-provided 2026-07-06 — legend '<3 ft Below Recommended'. Corroborated: American Whitewater #2921 'above 3 ft = at its best'.
Eddy gauge display (NOAA Gauge Details), 2026-07-06 — legend '3–4 ft Low Runnable'.
Eddy gauge display (NOAA Gauge Details), 2026-07-06 — legend '4–5 ft Medium Runnable' (the sweet spot); overall recommended Range 3–6 ft.
Eddy gauge display (NOAA Gauge Details), 2026-07-06 — top of '4–5 ft Medium Runnable'.
Eddy gauge display (NOAA Gauge Details), 2026-07-06 — legend '5–6 ft High Runnable', '>6 ft Above Recommended'. Corroborated: paddler D-bridge rule keys off gauge <6.0 ft.

characteristics.lowWaterMeaning: Below the runnable minimum the shut-ins are unrunnable rock (drag/portage). NUMERIC too_low anchor UNKNOWN — capture AW min from #2921.
characteristics.risingWaterHazards: Rain-driven flash rises turn Class II–IV into dangerous high water fast; pushy hydraulics in the shut-ins. High end can be lethal. NUMERIC high/dangerous anchors UNKNOWN — capture AW max + NWS ROZM7 flood stages.
characteristics.dropRateNote: Drops fast after a rise (pool-drop, small drainage) — capture specifics

localKnowledge: Only river in Missouri classified as whitewater; kayaked during spring high water (USFS Silver Mines).
Pool-drop character: swift stretches separated by calm pools (USFS).
Missouri Whitewater Championships (MWA) run this reach each spring; race is contingent on variable water levels — 'verify before traveling'.

sections.name: Lower Float (Silver Mines toward Sam A. Baker SP / Wappapello)
sections.description: Below the shut-ins the gradient eases; the river becomes a calmer float, notably slower below Sam A. Baker State Park (MCFA). Floated more like a normal Ozark stream. Candidate for a per-section river_type divergence from the whitewater default.

takeOut.name: Sam A. Baker State Park area / lower accesses

representativeGauge.rationale: Patterson gauge (co-located with NWS PAZM7) is the lower-river reference

characteristics.lowWaterMeaning: UNKNOWN — capture; low water mainly affects speed on this reach
characteristics.risingWaterHazards: Rises arrive from upstream rain; calmer than the shut-ins but still rain-responsive.

localKnowledge: MCFA notes the water is 'slower below' Sam A. Baker State Park (mile 66.5).

accessPoints.name: Millstream Gardens Conservation Area
accessPoints.notes: Whitewater put-in. S of Hwy 72 in Madison County, ~11 mi W of Fredericktown / ~11 mi E of Ironton. MCFA mile 20.2.
accessPoints.description: MDC conservation area (915.9 acres) in Madison County, ~8 mi west of Fredericktown, that serves as the put-in for the St. Francis River whitewater run through the Tiemann Shut-ins - Missouri's only Class II-IV whitewater. Paddlers carry down a steep slope to a gravel ramp on river-left; the run from here to Silver Mines is about 2.3 miles at ~35 ft/mile gradient.
accessPoints.parkingInfo: Designated graveled/paved parking lot off the access road; vehicle use restricted to established parking areas.
accessPoints.roadAccess: South of Highway 72 in Madison County (Route D from the south).
accessPoints.facilities: Picnic areas, hiking trails/boardwalk to the Tiemann Shut-ins overlook, and an archery range; primitive carry-down put-in (no developed ramp).
accessPoints.coordinateNote: OSM conservation-area centroid. The actual whitewater put-in is a gravel ramp at the river (river-left, ~MCFA mile 20.2) within the area, offset a few tenths of a mile south of this centroid; exact ramp not separately sourced.

accessPoints.name: Silver Mines Recreation Area (Hwy D)
accessPoints.ownership: U.S. Forest Service - Mark Twain National Forest
accessPoints.notes: Whitewater take-out / lower put-in. MCFA mile 23.2. USFS campground + day-use (loop count unverified).
accessPoints.description: USFS recreation area on the St. Francis River off Highway D in the Mark Twain National Forest, serving as the take-out for the Millstream Gardens whitewater run (MCFA mile 23.2) and a put-in for lower floats. The Highway D bridge and former Silver Mine dam are here. Named for the historic Einstein silver mine.
accessPoints.parkingInfo: Paved/gravel parking spurs at campsites and two day-use areas.
accessPoints.roadAccess: Highway D, ~2.9 mi south of MO Highway 72 near Oak Grove (from Fredericktown, west on Hwy 72 ~4.3 mi to Hwy D).
accessPoints.facilities: 70 campsites in four loops (11 with 30-amp electric), two day-use areas, ADA vault toilets, drinking water, picnic tables and fire rings; no showers or dump station.
accessPoints.coordinateNote: Official USFS recreation-area reference point off Highway D; the exact Hwy D bridge take-out/put-in is within a few hundred feet.

accessPoints.name: Sam A. Baker State Park access
accessPoints.notes: MCFA mile 66.5; water slower below here.
accessPoints.description: St. Francis River access within Sam A. Baker State Park (Wayne County). Below this point (MCFA mile 66.5) the river becomes slower, flatter float water. The park offers camping, hiking trails, a dining lodge and a camp store.
accessPoints.roadAccess: Via Missouri Route 143 off Highway 34, Wayne County (north of Patterson).
accessPoints.facilities: State park with campgrounds, trails, dining lodge, store and cabins.
accessPoints.coordinateSource: https://paddling.com/paddle/locations/sam-a-baker-state-park (St. Francis access coordinate); latitude cross-checked against OSM park centroid 37.2572
accessPoints.coordinateNote: paddling.com St. Francis access coordinate; latitude matches the OSM park centroid (37.2572) with an eastward shift to the river. Park frontage spans several river miles, so the exact MCFA-mile-66.5 put-in within the park may vary by up to ~1 mi.

accessPoints.name: Hwy 34 bridge
accessPoints.ownership: Missouri DOT highway bridge (public road right-of-way)
accessPoints.description: St. Francis River access at the Missouri Route 34 bridge, about 3 miles east of Patterson (MCFA mile 69.8). USGS gaging station 07037500 ('St. Francis River near Patterson') is located at this bridge.
accessPoints.roadAccess: Missouri Route 34, ~3 mi east of Patterson (Wayne County).
accessPoints.coordinateSource: USGS gage 07037500 (at the MO-34 bridge), waterservices.usgs.gov/nwis/site site service
accessPoints.coordinateNote: USGS streamgage 07037500 'St. Francis River near Patterson' is located at the Missouri Route 34 bridge (~3 mi east of Patterson); decimal degrees from the USGS site service. Represents the bridge crossing/access.

accessPoints.name: Lake Creek
accessPoints.description: River-right access with a reported campground where Lake Creek enters the St. Francis River (MCFA mile 76.4), a short distance above the Highway 67 bridge - the last access before Lake Wappapello.
accessPoints.roadAccess: Near the Highway 67 crossing of the St. Francis River above Lake Wappapello; exact access road unconfirmed.
accessPoints.facilities: Campground reported at the access (per MCFA/float guide); details unconfirmed.
accessPoints.coordinateSource: none - OpenStreetMap/GNIS candidates only, not confidently resolved
accessPoints.coordinateNote: No authoritative coordinate found for the 'Lake Creek' access/mouth. GNIS services were unavailable; OSM shows candidate right-bank tributaries in the correct latitude band near mile 76.4 - 'Center Lake Creek' (~37.118, -90.524) and 'Big Lake Creek' (~37.108, -90.512) - but the exact mouth could not be confirmed. Per float guides it lies on river-right a short distance above the Highway 67 bridge (~mile 78.6), the last access before Lake Wappapello. Safe to interpolate from mile 76.4.
